mentions.detail: One of the larger auto-ancillaries, doing extremely well with a monopoly in AC systems; hold and, if possible, add.

mentions.note: EV two-wheeler; very resilient. Add slowly on dips; petrol price hikes post-election could boost EV space.
Add slowly on dips; strong stock even in this market.
mentions.detail: Add slowly on dips; a very strong stock even in the current market.

mentions.note: Prefers Ather over Ola as of now
mentions.detail: Asked Ola vs Ather (viewer Surya), Kutumba Rao's preference is Ather as of now. Ola may see a short-term move but Ather looks fundamentally better. On Ola, wait for results before gaining confidence since management always speaks bullishly.

mentions.note: Recent listing, strong, holding above 1,000; hold.
mentions.detail: Recent listing; performing strongly, holding above 1,000 since listing; hold.

mentions.note: Range-bound 175-225 since listing, result below expectations, weakish; avoid for now.
mentions.detail: Range-bound roughly 175-225 since listing; result not as great as expected and stock trading weakish since; would avoid for now.

mentions.note: Insurance broking; ran 80->128 in 6 months; one to watch, good performance ahead.
mentions.detail: Insurance-broking company; stock ran from 80 to 128 over six months; promoter indicated good performance going forward; one stock to watch.

mentions.note: Chip-design company, lumpy revenue but a quarter can jump; hold with 1-2 yr view, good returns.
mentions.detail: Chip-design company, lumpy quarterly revenue but a quarter can jump; no other such listed name; design-outsourcing tailwind (Tata, Kaynes, CG Power). Hold with a 1-2 yr / long-term view; good returns expected. Don't panic.

mentions.note: Weak, near 52-wk low, top line flat; exit on rallies, technical bounce to ~360 possible.
mentions.detail: Weak, exit on rallies; near 52-wk low; result only slightly better, top line flat; technical rally to ~360 possible - exit near there.

mentions.note: Very average result, travel co; speculative, exit on any rally unless tomorrow's result surprises.
mentions.detail: Result very average, travel company -> speculative bet, exit on any rally; check tomorrow's result, hold only on a positive surprise.

mentions.note: Demerged shares already credited; listing date not yet announced - revisit after listing.
mentions.detail: Demerged shares already credited to accounts; listing hasn't happened, listing date not yet announced - revisit after listing.

mentions.note: Strong business but stretched valuations; add only on dips/sharp declines, slowly.
mentions.detail: Strong, thriving business but valuations stretched; add only on dips/sharp declines, slowly - not a buy at this price.

mentions.note: Operator-driven, rigging more than performance
mentions.detail: Said there is an operation/rigging by an operator; above 300 it has distributed heavily. He wouldn't be surprised if it fell to 200. The sector and order book are good, but as a stock be wary; only sustains if it trades and holds above 280 on volume.

mentions.note: Speculative but decent company, strong order book; flattish last qtr, watch next couple quarters but a stock to hold.
Good result, splendid expansion; possible placement ~400 -> further appreciation possible.
mentions.detail: Good result (+~20% since), splendid expansion plans; possible placement ~400 -> further appreciation possible.

mentions.note: Earlier 'dark horse'; hold long-term
mentions.detail: He had flagged it earlier as a dark horse and it performed reasonably well. Even in a volatile market it held above 40. Presently you can hold; it was a long-term pick.

mentions.note: Good bank-space stock; add carefully, no hurry
mentions.detail: He said you can add but there is no hurry since the market is weak and may fall further. It is a good stock in the banking space; watch the entry point carefully.

mentions.note: Buy/accumulate, no need to wait for a fall; add on dips
mentions.detail: Buy SBI 100%, no need to look back and forth. Recommended repeatedly for almost three years, including shifting out of Yes Bank. If bought at the high and it dips, average it. In the long term SBI will cross the 1,500 level and stabilize; it is a bank, no doubt. Accumulate and keep adding on dips.

mentions.note: Part of well-chosen long-term basket
mentions.detail: Part of a viewer's long-term basket (DCB Bank, L&T, SBI, Jio Finance, Dr Reddy's Labs). He approves the selection; continue holdings and definitely invest fresh.

mentions.note: Start slowly near 900, no rush
mentions.detail: Definitely start slowly but with no rush. The broader market will be weak and bank stocks usually suffer in higher inflation. After it comes close to 900, start accumulating SBI slowly.

mentions.note: Hold if already bought; no fresh buy now
mentions.detail: If already bought, hold. But fresh buying immediately is not necessary.

mentions.note: Exit on rallies; Delhi-centric, litigation
mentions.detail: Exit on rallies. It is not a pan-India hotel chain, only Delhi-centric (Asian Hotels North). It already ran to about 400 and is forming lower tops and lower bottoms, with news-driven activity and a litigation on the hotel's shopping arcade. Sell around 320 to 330; you should exit this counter.

mentions.note: Hold; correction likely short, can rebound
mentions.detail: Hold; all power companies irrespective of short-term performance he says hold. Decent Gujarat-based large group. It corrected modestly from around 1,800 to the support level near 1,450 on a weak result, but the correction is likely short and it could jump again. Don't sell at this level.

mentions.note: Hold with SL ~1500; richly valued PE ~90
mentions.detail: Caller bought near the peak (~2,000) after a ~1,000 rise in a month. Hold with a stop-loss around 1,500; if the stock rebounds, exit close to his price. It is a good stock without doubt but very richly valued at about PE 90.

mentions.note: Hold; no fresh buying, exit on rallies
mentions.detail: If you ask him, just hold; no fresh buying and exit on rallies. It stood strong and some buying comes in the counter at lower levels. If you have it, hold.

mentions.note: Infra space could rebound; hold
mentions.detail: Overall the infra space could see a rebound, there is a possibility, so he said hold.

mentions.note: Very weak; exit on rallies
mentions.detail: Morepen is very weak. He said exit on rallies.

mentions.note: Exit; jewelry best-of-times over, can fall
mentions.detail: He thinks jewelry companies have seen the best of times. Near a recession, even if margins are protected, sales improvement may not come. If you ask him, exit, because the stock can go down to 3,600 to 3,700 in the next one to two months.

mentions.note: Avoid; near 52-wk low, margins weak
mentions.detail: Good debt-free result but top line grew while bottom line and margins did not, so the stock did not rise and is near its 52-week low. He would not invest; the fintech field is competitive and unless they upgrade services the stock is hard to sustain. If already bought put a 200 stop-loss, else exit on rallies at 240 to 250.

mentions.note: Hold if held; no fresh buy, fully priced
mentions.detail: One of the larger, near-monopoly players in wheels, but now fully priced and the overall market is dull. If you have bought the stock, hold; fresh buying, no.

mentions.note: Operated counter, PE ~100; would not buy
mentions.detail: More of an operated/event-driven counter than a fundamental one; one should only hold if you follow the news regularly. It was once a penny stock and now trades close to 200 at about PE 100. He would not buy.

mentions.note: Weak numbers; avoid amid volatile crude
mentions.detail: Numbers came yesterday and were weak. For now, avoid. With volatile crude prices it is doubtful HPCL can perform.

mentions.note: Margins down, spreads to fall; near 52-wk low
mentions.detail: Margins fell a lot and a regulator order will compress their spreads further from the coming year. Despite higher volumes the stock trades near its 52-week low. He would avoid.

mentions.note: Wait and watch; treasury-loss risk on yields
mentions.detail: As of now, wait and watch. As yields rise, treasury losses for banks could also rise going forward.

mentions.note: Short-term weak but can rebound; hold
mentions.detail: Hold. Only because of short-term concerns there is a bit of weakness, but it can rebound. On the rebound, when it comes close to the caller's price, exit.

mentions.note: Decent March results; ran 24-25 to 32 in 5 days, wait for a correction then buy a little; fully paid-up.
mentions.detail: Decent March results, ran from 24-25 to 32 in five days. Wait for a correction (~30) then buy a little. Fully paid-up stock, don't dismiss as cheap because it's Rs 30. Worth investing.

mentions.note: Avoid; more downside likely
mentions.detail: Avoid as of now. Even at current levels he can't rule out more downside. The stock already tested 250 and rebounded to 330-340, but failed to cross there and fell again.

mentions.note: Avoid; best of railway-wagon cycle is over; lower-top/lower-bottom, only on a relief rally now.
mentions.detail: Best of the time for railway wagon companies is over (post aggressive Vande Bharat expansion '23-'24); lower-top lower-bottom, now ~280-290 on a relief rally. Avoid, no need to buy for now.

mentions.note: Asked alongside Vimta/Finotex for 2-3 year view; covered under accumulate answer, no negative flagged.
mentions.detail: Asked for a 2-3 year view alongside Vimta Labs and Finotex; covered under the general accumulate answer, no separate negative raised.

mentions.note: Weak results, rising luggage competition, near 52-wk low; needs reinvention to regain share.
mentions.detail: Weak results, competition rising in luggage with new brands pulling share, stock near 52-week low. Difficult to regain lost market share unless it reinvents/rebrands. I would avoid it.

mentions.note: Hold/accumulate; treat as a diversified engineering/defense stock, not just autos
mentions.detail: Asked by a viewer holding M&M at Rs 1700, Kutumba Rao said keep it because M&M also has defense exposure and is a diversified engineering stock, not just automobiles. He said hold, fresh buying is fine, and if asked, he would accumulate.

mentions.note: Buy immediately, at least a 1-year view.
mentions.detail: M&M - buy immediately, at least with a one-year view.

mentions.note: Good last-quarter and full-year results; data-centre/new segments opening up. Add slowly on dips.
mentions.detail: Add slowly on dips. Good last quarter and full-year results. Now caters to data centres / new segments beyond just retail and corporate; markets opening up.

mentions.note: Add slowly on dips; good last-quarter and full-year results; new demand from data centres beyond retail/corporate.
Hold as dark horse with stop-loss; Rs 1L cr funding + SBI package, Birla back in mgmt, but Bharti/Jio pressure.
mentions.detail: Hold (bought at 1.50). Rs 1 lakh crore funding plan over 3 years + Rs 35,000 cr SBI-led package; Kumar Mangalam Birla back in management is positive. But Bharti/Jio competition (Jio IPO ahead) means tough times. Treat as a dark horse with a stop-loss.

mentions.note: Short-term impact only; long term much better; near 52-wk low, negatives discounted. Hold; accumulate ~1,750.
mentions.detail: Short-term impact on market share/product range; long term company will do much better. Already near 52-week low, most negatives discounted. Hold if owned; if buying, start accumulating close up to 1,750 (figure as spoken).

mentions.note: Betting-policy euphoria fizzled; only for high risk-appetite investors.
mentions.detail: Avoid. Stock shows some momentum on the government betting policy/guidelines but the initial euphoria/exuberance has fizzled. Only for those with high risk-appetite and capacity; such business-model stocks not suitable for everyone.

mentions.note: Stay invested; slight recovery path, sustains above 475; no clear rationale to switch to Kaynes.
mentions.detail: Stay invested with Newgen. In a slight recovery path; recently went 500-plus and stays above 475 even after correcting. Doesn't understand the rationale for switching from Newgen to Kaynes.

mentions.note: Hold from IPO; sees upside to Rs 3,500-3,600
mentions.detail: Advised a viewer holding since the IPO to hold. MTAR touched a 52-week high of 2,844 and trades around 2,805-2,810 after an excellent consolidated result (~100% jump). He feels it can go up to Rs 3,500-3,600 thanks to its Bloom-Energy-type product and data-centre supplies, and called it the best of the Chennai recommendations.

mentions.note: Good choice; has even outperformed in this market.
mentions.detail: Buy; MTAR has even outperformed in this market, we can observe.

mentions.note: Preferred data-centre supplier play; makes parts for US partner, feeding data centres indirectly.
mentions.detail: More bullish on data-centre suppliers than operators (operator business risks becoming commoditised). MTAR on the radar — makes some parts for its US partner, indirectly feeding data centres.

mentions.note: Top pick of the four 'Technologies'; at a temporary peak, buy only on corrections.
mentions.detail: Number one choice among Nazara/Kaynes/Netweb/MTAR. Don't buy at current levels - reached a temporary peak. Buy after a correction; best on dips.

mentions.note: Top pick among four 'Technologies' stocks; accumulate nicely on every dip.
Hardware/server maker, not an IT software firm
mentions.detail: On Network Technologies/Netweb he stressed it is a hardware company supplying servers and high-end computing solutions (and some software) to IT firms, so it should not be valued like software companies. It took a hit in the recent tech selloff (had gone to ~4,500, back to ~3,600), but results are good and there is no need to panic — hold.

mentions.note: Good selection; start in tranches
mentions.detail: Part of the Polycab/NetWeb/Airtel question. He said the stock selection is good, so start buying little by little in tranches.

mentions.note: His pick of the four; good returns from current levels
mentions.detail: Among the caller's four stocks he would take Netweb. Called it a slightly risky bet but one that should give good returns from current levels. Even at a high PE (~110-112), once recovery kicks in the stock should definitely do much better.

mentions.note: Hold for now; last quarter very strong, if replicated price sustains with chance of more upside.
Second choice; accumulate on every dip along with MTAR.
mentions.detail: Second choice after MTAR. Accumulate nicely on every dip, along with MTAR.

mentions.note: Second pick among the four; accumulate nicely on every dip.
Decent March-quarter and full-year results; add slowly on declines around 2,500, Rs 100 below, staggered.
mentions.detail: Add slowly on declines in the 2,500 range. Decent March quarter and full-year results. Around 2,500 and Rs 100 below, in a staggered manner on dips.

mentions.note: A bit weakish currently but hold.
mentions.detail: Currently a bit weakish, but still hold.

mentions.note: Avoid until clarity; fertilizer crisis hurts imported-ore converters
mentions.detail: He said it is unfavourable because India mostly imports phosphate ore and then converts it, so even this resilient stock has started falling sharply, sliding from a stable Rs 130-140 in February to about Rs 110. Kharif season is starting and a good monsoon could help, but an uneven monsoon would hit fertilizer usage. As of now he would avoid until stability and clarity come.

mentions.note: Fell ~50% from high; fertiliser imports tough; better only if raw-material sourcing right, else likely stays weak.
mentions.detail: Fell by half from its high. Fertiliser imports getting tough. Would perform better only if raw-material sourcing is correct; otherwise likely stays weak. Phosphate is largely imported, processed here and sold.

mentions.note: Doubts for a 2-3 month bet; profit more than halved; needs deeper study, don't buy just because it fell.
mentions.detail: Has his own doubts for a 2-3 month bet. Fell 20%; profit dropped more than half (top line held). Needs deeper study whether one-off or sustained. Don't buy just because it fell; could fall further.

mentions.note: Buy in the offer-for-sale at the lowest point
mentions.detail: Buy in the offer-for-sale opening today at Rs 104 (5% discount to market), as it's the lowest point. Value is around Rs 100, and at ~20 PE it becomes an attractive valuation. It is a simple, steady finance company with a moat, although it was earlier over-hyped (rose from Rs 22-25 to 150-160 in the railway-stock frenzy).

mentions.note: Hold for dividend; 175 far off
mentions.detail: Said enjoy the dividend and keep holding; the rate of 175 rupees won't come soon, it has to come someday far off.

mentions.price: around Rs 80 would be better; below OFS price
mentions.note: Buy only for the dividend; weak optimism, trading below OFS price
mentions.detail: Optimism is weak; even the recent OFS got no response, and it trades about 7-8% below the OFS price. Railway stocks have become dividend stocks. If it comes near Rs 80 it would be better; watch the March-ending result, and if it sustains the company can report around Rs 7,000 crore-plus net profit and possibly raise the dividend. Buy only for that dividend; otherwise nothing else.

mentions.note: Hold temporarily; weak now but stable March results and dividend
mentions.detail: Weak as of now but the March results were stable and there is a dividend. No need to sell in a hurry in this weakness. If you must sell, sell ex-dividend.

mentions.note: Exit on rallies; weak
mentions.detail: Weak as of now. Exit on rallies.

mentions.note: Average; earnings-call developments could take it past 500
mentions.detail: Average here. There are a couple of developments in the company that were indicated on the earnings/conference call. If those play out, there are signs the stock will cross 500 again in the next two-to-three quarters.

mentions.note: Hold; decent March results, can recover to 480-500
mentions.detail: Temporarily weak but decent enough March results. If the market gets some stability, KPI Green should improve again with clear signs of moving closer to 480-500. No need to panic.

mentions.note: Exit/reduce on rallies; peak reached, flat result
mentions.detail: The stock has reached a peak; it touched over 1,100 and is forming lower tops and lower bottoms, and the result was flat. It already ran from ~500 to 700-plus recently, so it is better to reduce the position. Exit on rallies.

mentions.note: Exit at 180 on rallies for re-entry lower
mentions.detail: A good Kerala-based company (with a Warangal plant) but textiles are weak since US tariffs began, and it is already a heavyweight/expensive, stable-results name. The stock has likely bottomed, but a rally from here depends on cotton prices. Exit on rallies at 180 for a re-entry at lower levels.

mentions.note: Hold until results (~24th of the month)
mentions.detail: Stabilizing/slightly declining only because of impending results (date thought to be around the 24th). The new subsidiary Capstone Home Services Pvt Ltd's impact will not be felt immediately. Hold until results; no need to be hasty.

mentions.note: Hold; hyper-speculative activity, protect profits
mentions.detail: There is hyper-speculative activity and huge continuous volumes in the stock, which has to be understood. Hold as of now; if bought at lower levels, keep it as a trading stock to protect profits.

mentions.note: Result okay-not-great; back in buying range
mentions.detail: Yesterday's result wasn't great but is okay. The renewable space gets on-and-off interest and the stock has come below 50, into the buying range. Hold.

mentions.price: 48-50 range; decisive cross of 54-55 for big move
mentions.note: Buy with patience; good valuation, renewables demand strong
mentions.detail: Definitely buy at a decent valuation; results came with top line sustained and ~Rs 300 cr profit even after removing a one-off. Renewable space has continuous demand, so no fear. Stock will consolidate in the 48-50 range and rise slowly given high float (promoter only ~12%, public+institutions ~88%); a decisive cross of 54-55 can bring a big move.

mentions.note: Stagnant near 50 on institutional supply
mentions.detail: He said hold Suzlon at current levels. Due to institutional supply the stock has stagnated around 50 and needs a breakout from there before it can move higher.

mentions.note: Buy with medium-term view; recent correction complete, can hold above 50.
mentions.detail: Definitely buy if you have a medium-term view; recent correction is complete, stock can stay steady above 50.

mentions.note: Hold without doubt; stabilizing above 50
mentions.detail: Interest will return to it as the range opens up. The stock is now stabilizing above 50, in the 54-55 range. Hold, without any doubt.

mentions.note: Accumulate near 640-650; demerger likely within 6 months
mentions.detail: He said the Vedanta demerger issue looks set to be completed within the next six months, so one can buy. The correction in non-ferrous metals will not be prolonged because renewable-driven demand will revive non-ferrous metals; Vedanta is one of the larger players. Whenever it closes around Rs 640-650 the stock should be accumulated.

mentions.note: Good dividend but runs at the promoter's whim; avoid for peace of mind.
mentions.detail: He said Vedanta gives a good dividend now, but the company runs at the promoter's whim, so leave such risky bets aside; for peace of mind the PSU dividend stocks are a better opportunity.

mentions.note: Add slowly before the demerger record date; post-demerger add again based on resulting entities (e.g. silver).
mentions.detail: Record date for the demerger has come; add slowly before the record date. After the demerger completes, add again depending on the resulting different companies (e.g. silver).

mentions.note: Looks a bit weak; demerger issue pending. Hold with caution.
mentions.detail: Vedanta looks a bit weak; there's a demerger issue too. Treated as hold-with-caution alongside the decent companies.

mentions.note: Buy in weakness for 2-3 yr view; demerger to Vedanta juniors, sum-of-parts decent returns; high dividend payer.
mentions.detail: Buy in this weakness for a 2-3 year view. Corporate restructuring/demerger to form 'Vedanta juniors'; on sum-of-the-parts basis decent-returns possibility. High dividend payer; governance concerns aside, hasn't given investors losses.

mentions.note: Buy/add; small negative (fire-accident court matters) will pass; restructuring + demerger ahead; big commodity player.
mentions.detail: Buy / can add. Small negative today (court matters from the fire accident) will pass over time; more corporate restructuring and a demerger exercise ahead; one of the bigger commodity players amid a strengthening commodity rally.

mentions.note: Buy only on dips below 300 / in 260-280; now a dividend play
mentions.detail: Recently demerged and now trading standalone. Treat it largely as a dividend play, since all the important units have been demerged. If at all you want to buy, buy below 300, or in the 260-280 band closer to it.

mentions.note: Exit on any rally to 1,000-plus; continuous weakness
mentions.detail: The stock has continuously fallen and displayed weakness even after results. It failed to cross the previous high of 1,125 (and the ~1,300 high), and fizzled out near 1,100. It is already at 925 with 1,000 breaking; on any rally to 1,000-plus, definitely exit. It could stay flat for a few days.

mentions.note: Buy as market consolidates, with a strict stop loss near 500
mentions.detail: Its earlier rise was on data-center hype; it went above 700 then corrected, and now shows some stability. Last result commentary said projects are on track, bringing renewed buying interest. It dipped below 500 over three months and has risen to about 550; as the market consolidates one can buy, but keep a strict stop loss around 500.

mentions.note: Don't sell at current levels; data-centre story, now at a decent valuation after recent volatility.
mentions.detail: At current levels you shouldn't sell, because recently the stock has seen a lot of ups and downs; positive on the data-centre story, and it is now available at a decent valuation (~436).

mentions.note: Hold; good fundamentals but won't outperform now
mentions.detail: A good stock with good fundamentals, but not one that can outperform the market at this time. It has the financial strength to perform over a period. Hold, but do not panic-sell in weakness.

mentions.note: Wait for results (~29th this month) before buying
mentions.detail: One of the good stocks, trading near a 52-week high, though expensive and not a high-volume name; it continues with stable results. Results are nearby (date ~29th this month), so look after results before buying.

mentions.note: Buy on any dip into 360-380; succession set up, returns to old glory over time
mentions.detail: Kutumba Rao said to watch Biocon and buy it on any fall, whenever the stock comes into the Rs 360-380 band. He reasoned that the promoter has set the stage for succession and will clearly nominate a successor, so over a period Biocon will return to its old glory.

mentions.note: His Dec investment idea; results today key to justify the sharp rally
mentions.detail: Kutumba Rao said he has more interest in MTR, which rose very sharply recently, because they have been promoting it as an investment idea since December. He said its results today are key to see whether the rally was justified, so he flagged it to watch around the result.

mentions.note: Terrific result, profit up over 200%, pan-India presence; might jump
mentions.detail: Kutumba Rao noted Satin Creditcare, which he had mentioned earlier, delivered a terrific result with profit jumping over 200% and a pan-India presence. He said the stock might jump, framing it as part of staying invested in fundamentally sound, cash-rich stocks.

mentions.note: EV/energy-transition theme; possible sustained mid-term rally
mentions.detail: Kutumba Rao flagged that companies like JBM Auto and Olectra may have a sustained mid-term rally because of the emphasis the PM placed on the EV space. He said if the crude-oil crisis continues, demand will lean toward EV vehicles and EV public-transport buses, making it a theme to follow carefully.

mentions.note: Hold; possible surprise move as investment-banker peers jump.
mentions.detail: Hold definitely; after the recent correction the stock is looking good again. Investment-banker companies are all jumping, so JM could too. Corrected from near 200 and is now stabilising near 140; if it crosses 150 levels a good move could come.

mentions.note: Coal India subsidiary; only one result so far, definitely hold.
mentions.detail: This is a recently listed company, a subsidiary related to Coal India. Only one result has come so far, so it must definitely be held.

mentions.note: Not very positive on agro-chemicals now; monsoon/El Nino risk.
mentions.detail: Agro-chemical companies with higher raw-material costs will see margins fall; companies with lower raw-material costs will rise. This quarter's performance could be weak because of monsoon and the El Nino effect. So not very positive on agro-chemical companies as of now.

mentions.note: Some revival after touching bottom; hold for now.
mentions.detail: For now I'd say hold Trent, because we're seeing some revival in the stock after it touched bottom.

mentions.note: Weak, making losses; don't average, wait for turnaround.
mentions.detail: We're tracking it, but the stock is weak - a chemical-space company that, after re-listing, saw institutional activity rise then fade, and it is now making losses too. Don't average; wait for a turnaround in performance. It's a large company (almost a couple of thousand crores turnover).

mentions.note: Already ran to 140, now stabilizing; can cross 52-week high but depends on pending March results.
mentions.detail: Rico Auto has already risen a lot - it went up to 140 and is now stabilizing around 116-120. Hold. It can cross its 52-week high again, but that depends on results; the March results haven't come yet, so take a decision after they come.

mentions.note: PSU safety-net, good dividend payout
mentions.detail: He said accumulate Coal India anywhere close to 400 or 410, medium-to-long term. PSU stocks act as a safety net with sound performance; subsidiary IPOs and the announced government disinvestment pattern are positives, and there is a good dividend payout. It rose ~3% the prior session.

mentions.note: Not falling; fine to hold long-term, can be used.
mentions.detail: Coal India isn't falling at all; it's fine. Hold 100% for the long term.

mentions.note: Long-term dividend play; hold, don't panic-sell
mentions.detail: Viewer Sheshasai bought at Rs 449. Buy/hold only with a 2-5 year outlook; if you panic over a 10-20 rupee fall, don't touch it. It is a dividend play plus some capital-appreciation expectation, suited to low-risk-appetite investors. It rallied strongly and pulled back with volatility but will slowly recover - no worry if 5-10% down. Hold for the long term, not for short-term moves.

mentions.note: Recently-listed Coal India subsidiary; got listing hype, pulled back, now recovering slowly. Good product demand.
mentions.detail: This is a recently-listed Coal India subsidiary (the caller's 'Bharat Coal'). At listing it got good hype and rose swiftly, then came back, and is now slowly recovering. Their products all have good demand, so hold it for some time.

mentions.note: Stuck in 400-450 range; real action needs cross of 430-440. One of the better premium hotel stocks.
mentions.detail: Hold as of now, with a stop loss around 380. The stock has been fixed in roughly a 400-450 range since listing (it spiked to 465-470 once); real action needs a cross of 430-440 levels. It's one of the better hotel stocks - extreme-premium segment, so the economy has little effect on them - and this high-PE stock can surprise.

mentions.price: 590 (buy price); buy on dips, not at current rate
mentions.note: Has risen a lot; don't add at this rate, accumulate on dips. Good turnaround performance visible.
mentions.detail: Alu Fluoride has risen a lot; don't add at this rate - take it when some dips come. A good turnaround performance is visible in the stock.

mentions.note: Hold, no rush to add; range-bound till numbers improve
mentions.detail: Recovered from ~450 to ~660. Needs the flat March-quarter performance to improve in the June-ending quarter for a jump; otherwise range-bound roughly 600-700, with real strength only above 700. No need to buy aggressively here.

mentions.note: Avoid temporarily until results; APM gas/margin hit
mentions.detail: The gas-supply crisis has cut APM gas availability, and APM distribution margins are clearly showing signs of being affected. The stock is near its 52-week low (~165). He would avoid it temporarily until the result; there is no hurry.

mentions.note: Preferred over Yes Bank; shift Yes Bank holdings into SBI.
mentions.detail: Recommended as the shift destination from Yes Bank.

mentions.note: Accumulate

If it is only a small rally, exit; no one should buy fresh at this price. After the rights issue is done, look at the price and quarterly performance before deciding.

mentions.note: Avoid; exit on rallies
mentions.detail: Not a great stock; it moves in a 30-40 range on and off every 3-4 months, but has fallen continuously since the last quarter's result, going below 75 and bottoming near 87-90. Top line rose but bottom line did not, and raw-material/commodity costs are likely to rise. He would avoid it and exit on rallies.

mentions.note: Accumulate from current levels for the long term; can give decent returns.
mentions.detail: Caller asked about entering at the current price point. Kutumba Rao said buy/accumulate from current levels for the long term; can give decent returns. By long term he means hold a minimum of more than one year (don't ask back in three or six months).

mentions.note: Large dividend payer; add on a pullback for dividend yield.
mentions.detail: Mentioned alongside Canara Bank as a large dividend payer. With PSU banks slightly high now, attempt on a fall for dividend yield above the FD level.

mentions.note: Good niche engineering company; slow and steady, can go back up to 450-500. Hold.
mentions.detail: Caller holds 5,000 shares around 550. A good company with good market share on the engineering side, pumps for a niche segment. No immediate jump expected, but slow and steady it can go back up to 450-500 easily, so hold, no worry. When buying such large quantity, buy in tranches.

mentions.note: Don't chase; sustains only with follow-up buying. Mandatory stop-loss above 600.
mentions.detail: Caller bought around 600 in a hurry. Buying it repeatedly is unnecessary; it made a large move (crossed 600, up to ~620) but only sustains with follow-up buying, and fell sharply after results (bottomed ~475 over three months). If buying above 600, definitely keep a trading stop-loss; entering at higher levels is risky. The Thursday spike likely came on an unconfirmed social-media trigger.

mentions.note: Hold; becoming attractive at current levels
mentions.detail: Results are all good. Like other PSU shipyards many bought the 2024 peak and got stuck; touching old highs will take time. Becoming attractive to buy around 2,400-2,500. Don't sell, hold.

mentions.note: Hold cautiously; watch margins/acquisition
mentions.detail: Dec-quarter top line jumped but margins are disappointing; cash-rich and many await an acquisition that could take it back to 700-800 levels, but if cash exhausts without one there is downside risk. They paid Rs 12 cr interest (took a loan) which must be watched. Hold; watch one more quarter; SaaS model faces possible AI disruption.

mentions.note: Accumulate on every decline
mentions.detail: Buy without any doubt; accumulate on every decline. Post the REC merger this becomes one very big entity. Definitely a good buy at current levels.

mentions.note: Avoid; exit on rallies
mentions.detail: IPO-euphoria encashment; a ~Rs 7,000-8,000 cr market cap is not justified for a fashion-jewellery company of this size. Already at its 52-week low and could halve from here. It is now a large-player game (Aditya Birla's Indriya). Exit on rallies.

mentions.note: Hold; exit on rallies
mentions.detail: Good branding but flat and not growing, turnover stagnant for long; same risk as BlueStone applies. Watch whether the company restructures and kick-starts the business with new brands; otherwise it is a good takeover target. Exit on rallies.

mentions.note: Speculative bet only
mentions.detail: An extremely speculative counter, frequently in the ban list; swings to 175-180 then easily back to 135-140. Treat only as a speculative bet. At ~145-148 it can prepare a short-term rally and may attempt 160-180 again.

mentions.note: Wait one more quarter for fresh buying
mentions.detail: Corrected to ~3,300-3,400 after a brokerage report, recovered and now sustains at ~4,000; last quarter was flat. Would wait for another quarter of performance before fresh buying.

mentions.note: Buy nearer 22-23, in March
mentions.detail: HUL-demerged ice-cream entity, double down-circuit; market questions the ~Rs 6,000 cr market cap for an ice-cream company. Buyable closer to 22-23 levels, in March, with the summer season approaching.

mentions.note: Hold; low-liquidity caution
mentions.detail: A good company; hype has gone and it is now at a realistic valuation (~1,800-1,900 vs a prior 3,500). Results flat but competent, with an encouraging recent earnings-call transcript. Hold; watch carefully due to low volumes/liquidity which can cause sharp moves.

mentions.note: One of the better auto-ancillary names, decent results
mentions.detail: Kutumba Rao said hold Talbros Auto, calling it one of the better auto-ancillary companies with decent results. It is near its 52-week high (recently ~300, stable ~280) with signs of crossing the 52-week highs again, helped by auto ancillaries doing well lately.

mentions.note: Eye-popping Dec result + precious-metals rally
mentions.detail: He said hold Senco Gold. The December result was eye-popping: revenue ~Rs 3,000 crore (up 100% QoQ) and net profit from Rs 53 crore to Rs 267 crore. With 320-330 as a major base, the stock can attempt 400 again, helped by speculator fancy and the precious-metals rally.

mentions.note: Turnaround result, new high likely shortly
mentions.detail: He said buy TVS Supply Chain on dips. After good turnaround results the stock already rose ~25-30% from around 95-96 and a new high looks likely very shortly. Since the recent rally was sharp, he would buy on a small correction toward 107-108 over the next 1-2 months.

mentions.note: Former dark horse; controlled/operated stock
mentions.detail: He said if the holder wants to hold Sharath Industries, hold it. A former 'dark horse' (flagged at 40) on the aqua side that gave good returns earlier; recently dull with low volumes, around 170, close to its 52-week high. He cautioned it is more of a controlled/operated stock.

mentions.note: Poor recent results, promoter pledging; wait and see
mentions.detail: He advised waiting and watching rather than buying Tilaknagar Industries now. The recent results were poor (a loss despite a dramatic top-line jump and high capex), and promoters pledged maximum shares. He would wait to see whether a turnaround comes in the coming quarter before buying.

mentions.note: Bought near 52-week low; large pharma name
mentions.detail: He said hold Divi's Labs with nothing to fear. The caller bought near the 52-week low (~5,000; 52wk low 4,941) and the stock is already at ~6,100 (once went to 7,000). It is one of the better stocks to hold in an uncertain atmosphere, being one of the largest pharma market caps at Rs 1.6 lakh crore.

mentions.note: Fell with IT; nothing wrong company-specific
mentions.detail: On the caller's 50%-loss position, Kutumba Rao said it fell along with all IT stocks and there is nothing specifically wrong at the company. Recovery is likely only after IT sentiment improves (Ramakrishna added the chart is very weak with weak recent results).

mentions.note: Monopoly PSU; correction is a buying opportunity
mentions.detail: He said hold HAL, citing its monopoly status as a Government of India PSU counter. The current corrective mode is a buying opportunity, not a selling opportunity.

mentions.note: Exit on rallies; relief rallies not holding
mentions.detail: Recently controversial with news flow; even after clarifications the relief rallies are not holding up. Advised exiting on rallies. (Caller was in a 20% loss.)

mentions.note: Exit on rallies; weak December result
mentions.detail: December result was weak, below expectations, with a sharply lower bottom line. Advised exiting on rallies, even if it comes close up to 200.

mentions.note: Hold; transformative capex stage, fundamentally strong
mentions.detail: Company is in a transformative stage with heavy capex on new-generation products and lithium batteries; the Telangana unit is yet to be commissioned. Stock may stay weak near term with weak results, but fundamentally extremely strong, so hold; no need to sell at these levels.

mentions.note: Hold; recently listed, needs time
mentions.detail: Recently listed and needs more time frame. As of now, hold.

mentions.note: Hold; recently listed, needs time
mentions.detail: Grouped with the recently listed names; needs more time frame to play out. Hold for now.

mentions.note: Dark-horse holding/implementation company; news rallies fizzle
mentions.detail: Should be seen as a holding/implementation company. Anil Ambani group on-and-off news flow brings brief rallies that fizzle out immediately. Not a counter to pay much attention to; called it a dark-horse counter.

mentions.note: Partial exit; best of its time likely over
mentions.detail: Results are good, but he thinks the best of its time is over and it has reached a peak. Advised a partial exit at the 115-117 levels. Business may improve after peace/stability, but outperforming returns are not expected.

mentions.note: Buy only on declines; if held, trailing stop and hold
mentions.detail: Shows continuous height and action and has gained some fancy, but he treats it as a highly speculative bet (flat December result yet jumped from 160-165 to 190). Buy only on declines; if already bought, put a trailing stop and hold.

mentions.note: Wait; buy near major support 380-390 on weekly weakness
mentions.detail: At current levels (around 420-430) do not buy; wait. Recommended at 60-70 rupees three years back, it touched 500. Major support is around 380-390. Buy when that level comes during weekly weakness.

mentions.note: No fresh buying; book some profits
mentions.detail: At these levels, no fresh buying. Advised booking some profits since lower levels would have been available.

mentions.note: Hold; flagship med-equipment company
mentions.detail: A flagship medical-equipment company (not small) that performs well. Corrected to near its 52-week low on subdued recent results, but not a price to sell. Maybe exit on rallies once it reaches the 1,580-1,600 levels. (Caller was in ~50% loss.)

mentions.note: Hold; don't sell, P/E ~5 and high dividend
mentions.detail: May stay weak until the PFC merger ratio gets clarity and Government of India approval comes. Don't sell, though, because it trades at a P/E of about 5 (literally peanuts) and is a high-dividend payer. Take any decision only after total restructuring clarity. (Caller in ~50% loss.)

mentions.note: No buy now; enter ~75-76 after a further ~15% fall
mentions.detail: At current levels, don't buy. Already at its 52-week low with no great result, but maybe some value as a manufacturing company. Advised entering close to around the 75-76 levels, i.e. after roughly another 15% correction.

mentions.note: Add and hold; one of the better energy plays
mentions.detail: For a caller sitting on a 15-16% loss, he said you can both add and hold; no need to be afraid. Called it one of the better stars in the energy space with a strong acquisition portfolio that will grow well.

mentions.note: Buy after results (13th)
mentions.detail: Recently attempted 52-week highs and is modestly consolidating. Not a cheap stock at about 80 PE, but he expects a good result (tomorrow, the 13th) and advised buying after the result.

mentions.note: Exit on rallies; very weak
mentions.detail: Near a 52-week low with weak December-quarter results; he advised against averaging the caller's 50% loss. Financial performance is not promising at all, so exit on rallies.

mentions.note: High debt is working capital, no concern
mentions.detail: On a viewer's debt worry, he explained the debt is mostly working capital, normal for services firms. Borrowings of 175 crores against trade receivables of 185 crores, with neutral net cash flows; income growing and margins improving. Nothing to worry about, just invest and hold.

mentions.note: Accumulate on a modest dip
mentions.detail: Strong result; the stock hit 1,070, a 52-week high. He expects a modest correction toward 930-940 and said to accumulate at that level. Existing holders can hold.

mentions.note: Stay in Canfin, don't shift to RBL/AU
mentions.detail: Canfin is still strong with good post-result performance, so he would not suggest switching out of it (to RBL Bank or AU Small Finance Bank). Noted that AU, converting from small-finance to a regular bank and consolidating near 1,000, is momentum-wise currently the stronger name.

mentions.note: Speculative only; prefer frontline realty
mentions.detail: Said there are much better companies; treat it as speculative only or for those tracking inside info. Name changed to Valor Estate; came below 100 and mildly recovered on December results. Prefer frontline realty names like Brigade or Prestige.

mentions.note: Hold but trim above 25,900
mentions.detail: Held at 180; he suggested it yesterday too. As of now hold, but reduce the position when the market comes back above 25,900, because indications of crossing 26,000 are not appearing this series, which feels tight.

mentions.note: Hold; dividend stock near 52-week high
mentions.detail: Definitely hold; the holder (bought at 294, now ~270) is not in a big loss since the stock is near its 52-week high. Avail the second interim dividend (record date 12th). The stock will likely attempt 310-320 once, where the position can be trimmed. It is more of a dividend-paying stock with long-run capital appreciation too.

mentions.note: Accumulate slowly on dips toward 15,000
mentions.detail: Post-results it went to 17,000-plus, corrected, and is now around 15,200. Accumulate slowly from here; strong support has developed at 14,600-700, so buy whenever it comes closer to 15,000. As the country's largest carmaker it sustains profitability and margins and improved quarter-on-quarter.

mentions.note: Long-term buy only; better to enter after ex-rights
mentions.detail: If you have a long-term view, definitely buy, but no spectacular short-term gains. The stock corrected from ~800 to ~600 and the result was disappointing (slipped into a loss). With a proposed rights issue it will trade cum-rights, so it is better to think and take it after ex-rights.

mentions.note: Avoid; weak bottom line, only a retailer
mentions.detail: No positivity here. It is more of a retailer (not a manufacturer), so margins won't improve much, especially as e-commerce is taking sales even in fast-moving electrical goods. Profit came modestly (~Rs 29 cr) despite some top-line growth. If anything, exit even on a rally toward ~189.

mentions.note: Avoid for now; lower-top lower-bottom pattern
mentions.detail: For now avoid; it should be bought only on declines. The stock has formed lower-top, lower-bottom continuously for the last two quarters, so it won't jump easily. Maybe it reclaims 1,000 again; buy only if it crosses 1,000.

mentions.note: Hold but exit on bounce; narrow band 300-340, no immediate move to old 400+ highs.
mentions.detail: The worst is likely over but the stock moves in a narrow band between 300 and 340. The viewer bought at 340; that price will come easily and when it does, he says exit. He does not expect the old highs of 400-plus to come immediately.

mentions.note: Safest route if you can't bear individual-stock volatility (vs large-caps).
mentions.detail: Asked about replacing individual large-cap stocks with a Nifty ETF, he says ETF is a safe play. If you are in individual stocks and cannot bear their ups, downs and volatility, a Nifty ETF is the safest route - no doubt about it.

mentions.note: Hold, don't shift to Avantel yet; check result, may go 200+ after March then shift.
mentions.detail: Holder of 200 free shares asked about switching to Avantel. He says check the result first - Lokesh was hit indirectly by American tariffs and some sanctions, seen in the stock. The best of times are over and it corrected to 200, but after March there are signs of it going to 200-plus; shift at that time, not now.

mentions.note: Switch from PPL Pharma to Laurus Labs - no doubt.
mentions.detail: The viewer is at about a 15% loss in PPL Pharma and asked about switching to Laurus. He says from PPL to Laurus you can shift, no doubt about it.

mentions.note: Hold; first post-name-change quarterly given optimistically; good long-term returns.
mentions.detail: Hold Sequent Scientific. After the name change, the first conference was just the other day - this is the first-quarter result, given optimistically. The earnings-call transcript has to be seen, but in the long term it can give good returns.

mentions.note: Continue SIP; performing nicely.
mentions.detail: On Rekha's mutual-fund SIPs (Nippon India Small Cap, Kotak Flexi Cap, HDFC Large & Mid, Mirae Asset Large & Mid), he says they are fine - all performing nicely. Continue the SIPs as they give good returns in the long run.

mentions.note: Continue SIP; performing nicely.
mentions.detail: Part of Rekha's SIP set that he endorses continuing - all are performing nicely and give good long-run returns.

hdfclargeandmidcapfund.name: HDFC Large and Mid Cap Fund

mentions.note: Continue SIP; sometimes underperforms ICICI Pru/Motilal Oswal Large&Mid but good long-run.
mentions.detail: He says HDFC Large & Mid (and Mirae Asset) sometimes underperform compared to ICICI Prudential or Motilal Oswal Large & Mid, but otherwise in the long run they give good returns; continue the SIP.

miraeassetlargeandmidcapfund.name: Mirae Asset Large and Mid Cap Fund

mentions.note: Continue SIP; sometimes underperforms peers but good long-run returns.
mentions.detail: Part of Rekha's SIP set; sometimes underperforms ICICI Prudential or Motilal Oswal Large & Mid, but otherwise gives good long-run returns - continue.

mentions.note: Buy; ~13% dividend, parent toll revenue up 6500->7500 cr YoY; five toll assets + a HAM asset.
mentions.detail: Definitely buy IRB InvIT - no need to fear. The parent IRB Infra's toll revenues are strong and factorisation benefit accrues to this trust; toll revenue jumped year-on-year from 6,500 crore to 7,500 crore. It shows ~13% dividend, is near its 52-week high, and has five toll assets in Western and Southern India plus a HAM asset - looks stronger.

mentions.note: Hold if you have the time horizon; undervalued, deploys cash into equities; else switch to MF.
mentions.detail: Maithan Alloys is an undervalued company that deploys its cash into listed/unlisted equities rather than core business (it bought ~40,000-50,000 NSE shares at ~1,000, now doubled). If you have the time horizon, invest/hold; otherwise there are much better companies and a mutual fund is the better route.

mentions.note: Hold and wait for the 12th board-meeting (Dec) result; turnaround would help, else may retest 52-wk low.
mentions.detail: For a viewer at 352 now at a 50% loss, he says it disappointed since its hyped listing - even the September result was disappointing. There is a board meeting on the 12th for the December-quarter result; if a turnaround shows, the stock improves, otherwise it may retest the 52-week low. Having waited this long, it is better to hold and see the result.

mentions.note: Hold, don't book profit; group flagship in capital markets, near 52-week high - keep a trailing stop and let it run.
mentions.detail: For a viewer at average 120, he says hold and don't book profit. It is the group's flagship company in the capital markets and the stock is near its 52-week high. In stocks touching 52-week highs you should keep a trading stop, protect profits and let it run rather than selling just because it has risen.

mentions.note: Buy near 110; auto-ancillary, stock split done, liquidity up
mentions.detail: Rolex Rings is fine; it did a stock split and liquidity increased. Buy it only when it comes closer to 110. It is an auto-ancillary company, not comparable to broking names like Groww.

mentions.note: Avoid at current levels after 200% 6-month run
mentions.detail: The stock rose 200% in just six months (about 80 to 280) on speculation and is at 52-week highs. There are doubts it can rise further from these levels unless the company delivers another successful blockbuster VFX project. As of now do not buy; consider only on a strong decline.

mentions.note: Wait another quarter; weak Dec result, micro-cap
mentions.detail: Recently listed with only one quarter of performance, and the December result was not good at all, leaving the stock near its 52-week low around 130. Fundamentals do not seem to justify the valuation. It is a micro-cap (about Rs 850 crore market cap). Wait for another quarter.

mentions.note: Buy for short/short-medium term only, not long term
mentions.detail: The result has come and is good, but the stock is not a fancy one. Keeping that in view, it can be bought for the short or short-medium term. Long-term bets in such companies do not work well.

mentions.note: Hold; weak-looking but high potential, not cheap
mentions.detail: Hold; no need to fear. Performance may feel weak but potential is high, and such future-discounting stocks tend to move. It is not a cheap stock, around Rs 12,000 crore market cap. The December profit dip was a one-off, so no need to exit even if the stock is weak now.

mentions.note: Buy/accumulate; medium-term target 425-430
mentions.detail: Hold and buy. Earlier advised to accumulate even at 320-330, with a medium-term target of 425-430. Definitely a buy.

mentions.note: Avoid as investment; now a trading/F&O bet
mentions.detail: It has become an institutional stock since going into F&O, with swift 5-10% weekly moves on and off. It has become more of a trading bet than an investment bet as of now.

mentions.note: Even good Dec results couldn't lift the stock; exit on rallies
mentions.detail: The stock was at 520 in Sep-Oct and has halved; even after good December top-line and bottom-line results it couldn't rise, so 'we are missing out on something.' Avoid; if already held, exit on rallies around 310-320 and shift to a better-managed energy/transformer company like Hitachi Energy.

mentions.note: Record results (+90% net profit); buy on every dip
mentions.detail: Net profit rose 90% YoY with highest-ever revenue, EBITDA (180cr to 370cr) and PAT/PBT. He had recommended buying it on every dip; the stock opened strongly and is in upper circuit. He also flags it as the preferred switch destination from Transformers & Rectifiers and Kaynes.

mentions.note: Range-bound 120-140 absent game-changing news
mentions.detail: Range-bound 120-140 unless game-changing news comes. Glass showed ~23% growth and consolidated Q3 ~37% YoY, but PAT only rose ~26-27%. The bio-process-systems entry will take more time to outperform. 140 was the issue price; no need to sell or add - hold.

mentions.note: One of the 3-yr outperformers; exit only on rallies if needed
mentions.detail: Over the last three years it has surprisingly been one of the outperforming stocks and only came down a bit in mild corrections. A decent stock to hold; if one wants to exit, do so on rallies, but at this time hold. A good Bharti Group company.

mentions.note: Buy at attractive valuation; stock-picker's paradise now
mentions.detail: Asked by a viewer (Saira Sriram), he said to definitely lock it down and buy, as the stock now has an attractive valuation. He added that with the budget over and only the Indo-US deal ahead, it is now a stock-picker's paradise - favour companies that benefit indirectly from the budget or have completed expansion capex.

iciciprudentialnifty200fundbandhaninfras.name: ICICI Prudential Nifty 200 Fund / Bandhan Infrastructure Fund

mentions.note: Switch out of broader-market and infra themes into diversified funds
mentions.detail: A viewer (Nagarajan) was at a loss in ICICI Prudential Nifty 200 (broader-market) and Bandhan Infrastructure (sectoral). Kutumba Rao said negative sentiment is heavier in the broader market versus Nifty 50, and infra is unlikely to see any big move even post-budget. He advised exiting these as far as possible and moving into diversified flexi-cap or multi-cap funds, and warned against chasing gold/silver - asset allocation must be followed strictly.

mentions.note: Hold; demerger of medical-devices business could add momentum
mentions.detail: A viewer is at a ~42% loss. Given the big correction in mid- and small-cap stocks, he advised holding. Their medical-devices business is heading for a listing/demerger, and after that news there is a chance of some momentum in the stock.

mentions.note: Flat results; near 52-week low, hold for post-Budget infra push
mentions.detail: Somany's results were flat - top line and bottom line both flat with no big improvement, so no need to average now. Maybe hold for the Budget; if there is an infra push, building/construction/infra companies are likely to rise post-Budget. It is closer to the 52-week low, so it's not the time to sell.

mentions.note: Don't take a gold loan to invest; use savings only
mentions.detail: A viewer wanted to pledge his gold for a Rs 16 lakh loan to invest in Gold BeES. Kutumba Rao said no - never invest with borrowed money, use only savings. If gold corrects (possible within six months to a year) the leveraged position is risky; understand that risk before acting.

mentions.note: Hold all infra ahead of Budget; Dec results awaited
mentions.detail: He said to compulsorily hold all infra stocks ahead of the Budget, as it is a sector the government must address. Fundamentally a good counter, no doubt. September results were soft due to monsoon (lower execution); December results are still awaited but likely better with no monsoon. If asked, hold.

goldetftatagoldetfnippongoldbeesicicipru.name: Gold ETF (Tata Gold ETF / Nippon Gold BeES / ICICI Pru Gold)

mentions.note: Continue gold SIP/ETF; weak dollar and central-bank buying support gold
mentions.detail: On a viewer's gold-vs-Jio-Finance SIP question, Kutumba Rao framed them as different asset classes and advised allocating across both. He endorsed continuing or adding gold via ETF (Tata Gold, Nippon Gold BeES or ICICI Pru Gold), noting these gave roughly 95-100% returns over the last year. He argued a weakening dollar index and central banks shifting reserves from treasuries into gold support gold over the long term.
